This project has no memory yet. The current session will seed it; subsequent sessions will receive auto-injected context for relevant past work.

Memory injection starts on your second session in a project.

`/learn-codebase` is available if the user wants to front-load the entire repo into memory in a single pass (~5 minutes on a typical repo, optional). Otherwise memory builds passively as work happens.

Live activity: http://localhost:37777
How it works: `/how-it-works`

# claude-mem status

This project has no memory yet. The current session will seed it; subsequent sessions will receive auto-injected context for relevant past work.

Memory injection starts on your second session in a project.

`/learn-codebase` is available if the user wants to front-load the entire repo into memory in a single pass (~5 minutes on a typical repo, optional). Otherwise memory builds passively as work happens.

Live activity: http://localhost:37777
How it works: `/how-it-works`

attachment.content:
- ruflo-core:ruflo-status: Show Ruflo system health, MCP server status, and active agents
- ruflo-core:witness: Manage and verify a cryptographically-signed fix manifest with temporal history (ADR-103)
- ruflo-swarm:swarm: Initialize, monitor, and manage multi-agent swarms
- ruflo-swarm:watch: Live-stream swarm events and agent activity in real time
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-status: Quick autopilot progress summary with task completion stats
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot: Enable, configure, or disable autonomous task completion
- ruflo-federation:federation: Manage cross-installation agent federation
- claude-mem:babysit: Watch a pull request or review cycle until it is ready to merge. Use when asked to babysit, monitor, or keep checking PR comments, reviews, and CI until all actionable issues are resolved.
- claude-mem:learn-codebase: Prime a codebase by reading every source file in full. Use when starting work on a new or unfamiliar project, or when the user asks to "learn the codebase", "read the codebase", "prime", or "get up to speed".
- claude-mem:how-it-works: Explain how claude-mem captures observations, when memory injection kicks in, and where data lives. Use when the user asks "how does claude-mem work?" or "what is this thing doing?".
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create a detailed, phased implementation plan with documentation discovery. Use when asked to plan a feature, task, or multi-step implementation — especially before executing with do.
- claude-mem:knowledge-agent: Build and query AI-powered knowledge bases from claude-mem observations. Use when users want to create focused "brains" from their observation history, ask questions about past work patterns, or compile expertise on specific topics.
- claude-mem:timeline-report: Generate a "Journey Into [Project]" narrative report analyzing a project's entire development history from claude-mem's timeline. Use when asked for a timeline report, project history analysis, development journey, or full project report.
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- claude-mem:pathfinder: Map a codebase into feature-grouped flowcharts, identify duplicated concerns across features, and propose a unified architecture. Use when asked to "find the ideal path," unify duplicated systems, or audit architecture before a refactor. Emits a proposed unified flowchart plus per-system /make-plan prompts.
- claude-mem:smart-explore: Token-optimized structural code search using tree-sitter AST parsing. Use instead of reading full files when you need to understand code structure, find functions, or explore a codebase efficiently.
- claude-mem:version-bump: Automated semantic versioning and release workflow for Claude Code plugins. Handles version increments across package.json, marketplace.json, plugin.json manifests, npm publishing (so `npx claude-mem@X.Y.Z` resolves), build verification, git tagging, GitHub releases, and changelog generation.
- claude-mem:do: Execute a phased implementation plan using subagents. Use when asked to execute, run, or carry out a plan — especially one created by make-plan.
- claude-mem:wowerpoint: Turn one document into a kawaii NotebookLM slide-deck PDF. Use for "wowerpoint this", "make a deck about <file>", "turn this report into slides", or any request to render a single document as shareable narrative slides.
- ruflo-core:witness: Sign, verify, and track fix-marker regressions over time using a deterministic Ed25519 witness manifest. Works in any project — clone the toolkit, run init, register fixes, regen on each release.
- ruflo-core:discover-plugins: Discover and recommend ruflo plugins based on your workflow, installed MCP tools, and current task
- ruflo-core:ruflo-doctor: Run health checks on the Ruflo installation and fix common issues
- ruflo-core:init-project: Initialize a new Ruflo project with MCP tools, hooks, and agent configuration
- ruflo-swarm:swarm-init: Initialize a multi-agent swarm with anti-drift configuration
- ruflo-swarm:monitor-stream: Stream live swarm events using the Monitor tool for real-time observability
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-predict: Use learned patterns and current state to predict the optimal next action
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-loop: Run an autonomous /loop iteration -- check progress, work on next task, schedule next wake
- ruflo-federation:federation-audit: Query federation audit logs with compliance filtering
- ruflo-federation:federation-status: Show federation health — peers, sessions, trust levels, and message metrics
- ruflo-federation:federation-init: Initialize federation on this node — generate keypair and configure peers
- ui-ux-pro-max:ui-ux-pro-max: UI/UX design intelligence for web and mobile. Includes 50+ styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 10 stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and HTML/CSS). Actions: plan, build, create, design, implement, review, fix, improve, optimize, enhance, refactor, and check UI/UX code. Projects: website, landing page, dashboard, admin panel, e-commerce, SaaS, portfolio, blog, and mobile app. Elements: button, modal, navbar, sidebar, card, table, form, and chart. Styles: glassmorphism, claymorphism, minimalism, brutalism, neumorphism, bento grid, dark mode, responsive, skeuomorphism, and flat design. Topics: color systems, accessibility, animation, layout, typography, font pairing, spacing, interaction states, shadow, and gradient. Integrations: shadcn/ui MCP for component search and examples.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# 🚀 ProjectLauncher — Build a Desktop-Grade AI Project Manager with Claude Code
2	### A Complete Step-by-Step Guide using Task Master AI · Roo Code · MCP · Free AI APIs
3	
4	---
5	
6	## 📋 Table of Contents
7	
8	1. [What We're Building](#1-what-were-building)
9	2. [Prerequisites & System Requirements](#2-prerequisites--system-requirements)
10	3. [Install Claude Code & Recommended Extensions](#3-install-claude-code--recommended-extensions)
11	4. [Set Up Your Workspace Structure](#4-set-up-your-workspace-structure)
12	5. [Install Task Master AI (MCP)](#5-install-task-master-ai-mcp)
13	6. [Configure Your Free AI API (OpenRouter / NVIDIA / Gemini)](#6-configure-your-free-ai-api-openrouter--nvidia--gemini)
14	7. [Create the Electron Desktop App Shell](#7-create-the-electron-desktop-app-shell)
15	8. [Build the UI with Roo Code + UX Pro Max Design System](#8-build-the-ui-with-roo-code--ux-pro-max-design-system)
16	9. [Build the AI Project Scanner Engine](#9-build-the-ai-project-scanner-engine)
17	10. [Wire Up: Install Requirements Button](#10-wire-up-install-requirements-button)
18	11. [Wire Up: Start Project Button](#11-wire-up-start-project-button)
19	12. [Add the File/Folder Browser (Native OS Dialog)](#12-add-the-filefolder-browser-native-os-dialog)
20	13. [Task Master AI Workflow Integration](#13-task-master-ai-workflow-integration)
21	14. [Dot Mac (.mdc) Rules Configuration](#14-dot-mac-mdc-rules-configuration)
22	15. [Polish: Animations, Themes & Micro-interactions](#15-polish-animations-themes--micro-interactions)
23	16. [Test, Build & Package as a Desktop App](#16-test-build--package-as-a-desktop-app)
24	17. [Marketplace Extensions to Install](#17-marketplace-extensions-to-install)
25	18. [Final Folder Structure](#18-final-folder-structure)
26	19. [Troubleshooting](#19-troubleshooting)
27	
28	---
29	
30	## 1. What We're Building
31	
32	A **native desktop application** (Electron + React) that:
33	
34	- Lets you **click-browse** your PC for any project folder
35	- **AI-scans** the folder (read-only) using a free AI model via OpenRouter / NVIDIA NIM / Gemini
36	- Automatically **detects** `requirements.txt`, `main.py`, `app.py`, `manage.py`, `index.js`, etc.
37	- Exposes two clean buttons: **▶ Install Requirements** and **⚡ Start Project**
38	- Streams **live terminal output** inside the UI
39	- Is **fully themed** with a dark, industrial-futuristic aesthetic using Roo Code's design tokens
40	- Uses **Task Master AI** to manage the build process itself (so Claude Code builds this app step-by-step intelligently)
41	
42	**Stack:** Electron · React · Vite · Node.js · Python bridge · Task Master AI MCP · Free AI API
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 2. Prerequisites & System Requirements
47	
48	### Required Software
49	
50	```bash
51	# Check versions before starting
52	node --version       # Need v18+
53	npm --version        # Need v9+
54	python --version     # Need 3.8+
55	git --version        # Any recent version
56	```
57	
58	### Install Node.js (if not installed)
59	```
60	https://nodejs.org → Download LTS version (v20 recommended)
61	```
62	
63	### Install Python (if not installed)
64	```
65	https://python.org → Download 3.11+ (check "Add to PATH" on Windows)
66	```
67	
68	### Operating System
69	| OS | Status |
70	|---|---|
71	| Windows 10/11 | ✅ Fully supported |
72	| macOS 12+ | ✅ Fully supported |
73	| Ubuntu 20.04+ | ✅ Fully supported |
74	
75	---
76	
77	## 3. Install Claude Code & Recommended Extensions
78	
79	### Step 3.1 — Install Claude Code (CLI)
80	
81	```bash
82	npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
83	```
84	
85	Verify:
86	```bash
87	claude --version
88	```
89	
90	### Step 3.2 — Install VS Code (if you want the editor too)
91	
92	```
93	https://code.visualstudio.com
94	```
95	
96	### Step 3.3 — Essential VS Code Marketplace Extensions
97	
98	Install these in VS Code (`Ctrl+Shift+X` → search each name):
99	
100	| Extension | Publisher | Why You Need It |
101	|---|---|---|
102	| **Roo Code** | RooVeterinaryInc | AI-powered code agent with UX design awareness |
103	| **Tailwind CSS IntelliSense** | Bradlc | Autocomplete for Tailwind classes |
104	| **Prettier** | Prettier | Auto-formats all your code |
105	| **ESLint** | Microsoft | Catches JS/React bugs |
106	| **Python** | Microsoft | Python syntax + linting |
107	| **Auto Rename Tag** | Jun Han | Rename HTML/JSX tags together |
108	| **Path Intellisense** | Christian Kohler | Autocomplete for file paths |
109	| **Better Comments** | Aaron Bond | Color-coded TODO/NOTE/FIXME |
110	| **Error Lens** | Alexander | Inline error messages |
111	| **GitLens** | GitKraken | Git history inline |
112	| **vscode-icons** | VSCode Icons Team | Project file icons |
113	| **Thunder Client** | Ranga Vadhineni | Test APIs without Postman |
114	| **DotENV** | mikestead | `.env` file syntax highlighting |
115	
116	### Step 3.4 — Install Roo Code Extension
117	
118	In VS Code:
119	1. Press `Ctrl+Shift+X`
120	2. Search: `Roo Code`
121	3. Click **Install**
122	4. After install → click **Roo Code icon** in left sidebar
123	5. Set your preferred AI provider (we'll use OpenRouter — see Section 6)
124	
125	---
126	
127	## 4. Set Up Your Workspace Structure
128	
129	### Step 4.1 — Create the Project
130	
131	```bash
132	mkdir project-launcher
133	cd project-launcher
134	git init
135	```
136	
137	### Step 4.2 — Open in Claude Code
138	
139	```bash
140	claude
141	```
142	
143	This opens an interactive Claude Code session in your terminal. Claude can now read, write, and run code in this folder.
144	
145	### Step 4.3 — Open in VS Code as well
146	
147	```bash
148	code .
149	```
150	
151	You'll work in **both terminals** — Claude Code CLI for AI-driven tasks, VS Code for visual editing and Roo Code.
152	
153	---
154	
155	## 5. Install Task Master AI (MCP)
156	
157	Task Master AI breaks your big project into smart subtasks and manages them through Claude Code.
158	
159	### Step 5.1 — Install Task Master AI globally
160	
161	```bash
162	npm install -g task-master-ai
163	```
164	
165	### Step 5.2 — Configure MCP in Claude Code
166	
167	Create the MCP config file:
168	
169	```bash
170	# In your project root
171	mkdir -p .claude
172	```
173	
174	Create `.claude/mcp.json`:
175	
176	```json
177	{
178	  "mcpServers": {
179	    "taskmaster-ai": {
180	      "command": "npx",
181	      "args": ["-y", "--package=task-master-ai", "task-master-ai"],
182	      "env": {
183	        "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY": "your_anthropic_key_here",
184	        "OPENROUTER_API_KEY": "your_openrouter_key_here",
185	        "MODEL": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
186	        "MAIN_MODEL": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
187	        "RESEARCH_MODEL": "openrouter/google/gemini-flash-1.5",
188	        "FALLBACK_MODEL": "openrouter/meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct:free"
189	      }
190	    }
191	  }
192	}
193	```
194	
195	### Step 5.3 — Initialize Task Master in your project
196	
197	Inside Claude Code session (`claude` command):
198	
199	```
200	/mcp
201	```
202	
203	Confirm Task Master is connected. Then:
204	
205	```
206	Initialize taskmaster-ai for a new project called "ProjectLauncher"
207	```
208	
209	This creates a `tasks/` folder and `tasks.json` scaffold.
210	
211	### Step 5.4 — Create your PRD (Product Requirements Document)
212	
213	Create `scripts/prd.txt`:
214	
215	```
216	# ProjectLauncher — PRD
217	
218	## Overview
219	A desktop Electron app that lets users browse their PC for any Python/Node project folder,
220	uses a free AI API to scan the project (read-only), detects the entry point and requirements file,
221	then presents two action buttons: Install Requirements and Start Project.
222	
223	## Core Features
224	1. Native folder browser dialog (OS-level)
225	2. AI project scanner (OpenRouter free tier or Gemini free tier)
226	3. requirements.txt / package.json / pyproject.toml auto-detection
227	4. Entry point detection (main.py, app.py, manage.py, index.js, server.js)
228	5. Install Requirements button → runs pip install or npm install with live streaming output
229	6. Start Project button → runs detected start command with live streaming output
230	7. Dark industrial UI with animated terminal output panel
231	8. Persistent project history (last 10 projects)
232	
233	## Tech Stack
234	- Electron 28+ (desktop shell)
235	- React 18 + Vite (UI)
236	- Tailwind CSS (styling)
237	- Node.js IPC bridge (main ↔ renderer)
238	- child_process (running pip/npm/python commands)
239	- OpenRouter API or Gemini API (free AI scanning)
240	```
241	
242	Now ask Task Master to parse it:
243	
244	```
245	task-master parse-prd scripts/prd.txt
246	```
247	
248	Task Master will generate structured tasks. View them:
249	
250	```
251	task-master list
252	```
253	
254	You'll see numbered tasks like:
255	- Task 1: Set up Electron + React + Vite boilerplate
256	- Task 2: Build folder browser component
257	- Task 3: Build AI scanner module
258	- Task 4: Build terminal output panel
259	- etc.
260	
261	---
262	
263	## 6. Configure Your Free AI API (OpenRouter / NVIDIA / Gemini)
264	
265	You only need **ONE** of these. Pick the easiest for you.
266	
267	### Option A — OpenRouter (RECOMMENDED — Most Free Models)
268	
269	1. Go to: `https://openrouter.ai`
270	2. Sign up (free)
271	3. Go to **Keys** → Create API Key
272	4. Free models available:
273	   - `google/gemini-flash-1.5` — fast, smart, generous free tier
274	   - `meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct:free` — completely free
275	   - `microsoft/phi-3-mini-128k-instruct:free` — great for code analysis
276	
277	5. Copy your key: `[REDACTED_OPENROUTER_KEY]`
278	
279	### Option B — Google Gemini (Free Tier)
280	
281	1. Go to: `https://aistudio.google.com`
282	2. Sign in with Google
283	3. Click **Get API Key**
284	4. Free tier: Gemini 1.5 Flash — 15 requests/minute, 1M tokens/day
285	5. Copy your key: `[REDACTED_GOOGLE_KEY]`
286	
287	### Option C — NVIDIA NIM (Free Credits on Sign-up)
288	
289	1. Go to: `https://build.nvidia.com`
290	2. Sign up → get 1000 free API credits
291	3. Use models like `meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct`
292	4. Copy your key: `nvapi-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`
293	
294	### Create your `.env` file
295	
296	In project root, create `.env`:
297	
298	```env
299	# Pick ONE provider and fill it in
300	
301	# OpenRouter (recommended)
302	OPENROUTER_API_KEY=[REDACTED_OPENROUTER_KEY] OR Gemini
307	GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED_GOOGLE_KEY] OR NVIDIA NIM
311	NVIDIA_API_KEY=nvapi-your-key-here
312	NVIDIA_BASE_URL=https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1
313	NVIDIA_MODEL=meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct
314	
315	# App settings
316	MAX_SCAN_DEPTH=5
317	MAX_FILES_TO_SCAN=50
318	```
319	
320	> ⚠️ Add `.env` to your `.gitignore` immediately — never commit API keys.
321	
322	```bash
323	echo ".env" >> .gitignore
324	```
325	
326	---
327	
328	## 7. Create the Electron Desktop App Shell
329	
330	### Step 7.1 — Initialize the project
331	
332	```bash
333	npm create vite@latest . -- --template react
334	npm install
335	```
336	
337	### Step 7.2 — Install Electron and dependencies
338	
339	```bash
340	npm install --save-dev electron electron-builder concurrently wait-on cross-env
341	npm install electron-is-dev
342	npm install tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer
343	npm install @tailwindcss/typography framer-motion lucide-react
344	npm install dotenv axios

400	### Step 7.4 — Create the Electron main process
401	
402	Create `electron/main.js`:
403	
404	```js
405	const { app, BrowserWindow, ipcMain, dialog, shell } = require('electron');
406	const path = require('path');
407	const { spawn } = require('child_process');
408	const fs = require('fs');
409	const isDev = require('electron-is-dev');
410	
411	let mainWindow;
412	
413	function createWindow() {
414	  mainWindow = new BrowserWindow({
415	    width: 1200,
416	    height: 800,
417	    minWidth: 900,
418	    minHeight: 600,
419	    frame: false,         // Custom titlebar
420	    titleBarStyle: 'hiddenInset',
421	    backgroundColor: '#0a0a0f',
422	    webPreferences: {
423	      nodeIntegration: false,
424	      contextIsolation: true,
425	      preload: path.join(__dirname, 'preload.js'),
426	    },
427	    icon: path.join(__dirname, 'assets', 'icon.png'),
428	  });
429	
430	  mainWindow.loadURL(
431	    isDev
432	      ? 'http://localhost:5173'
433	      : `file://${path.join(__dirname, '../dist/index.html')}`
434	  );
435	
436	  if (isDev) mainWindow.webContents.openDevTools({ mode: 'detach' });
437	}
438	
439	app.whenReady().then(createWindow);
440	
441	app.on('window-all-closed', () => {
442	  if (process.platform !== 'darwin') app.quit();
443	});
444	
445	// ── IPC: Open folder dialog ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
446	ipcMain.handle('dialog:openFolder', async () => {
447	  const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow, {
448	    properties: ['openDirectory'],
449	    title: 'Select Your Project Folder',
450	  });
451	  if (result.canceled) return null;
452	  return result.filePaths[0];
453	});
454	
455	// ── IPC: Scan project folder (read-only) ─────────────────────────────────────
456	ipcMain.handle('project:scan', async (event, folderPath) => {
457	  const result = {
458	    folderPath,
459	    files: [],
460	    requirementsFile: null,
461	    entryPoint: null,
462	    projectType: null,
463	    packageManager: null,
464	  };
465	
466	  // Recursive file scanner (depth-limited, read-only)
467	  function scanDir(dir, depth = 0) {
468	    if (depth > 4) return;
469	    try {
470	      const entries = fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
471	      for (const entry of entries) {
472	        if (entry.name.startsWith('.') || entry.name === 'node_modules' ||
473	            entry.name === '__pycache__' || entry.name === 'venv' ||
474	            entry.name === '.venv' || entry.name === 'dist' ||
475	            entry.name === 'build') continue;
476	
477	        const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name);
478	        const relPath = path.relative(folderPath, fullPath);
479	
480	        if (entry.isFile()) {
481	          result.files.push(relPath);
482	
483	          // Detect requirements
484	          if (entry.name === 'requirements.txt') result.requirementsFile = relPath;
485	          if (entry.name === 'pyproject.toml' && !result.requirementsFile) result.requirementsFile = relPath;
486	          if (entry.name === 'package.json' && depth === 0) {
487	            result.requirementsFile = relPath;
488	            result.packageManager = 'npm';
489	          }
490	
491	          // Detect entry point
492	          const entryPriority = ['main.py','app.py','run.py','server.py',
493	                                  'manage.py','index.js','server.js','app.js','main.js'];
494	          for (const ep of entryPriority) {
495	            if (entry.name === ep && !result.entryPoint) {
496	              result.entryPoint = relPath;
497	              result.projectType = ep.endsWith('.py') ? 'python' : 'node';
498	            }
499	          }
500	        } else if (entry.isDirectory()) {
501	          scanDir(fullPath, depth + 1);
502	        }
503	      }
504	    } catch (e) { /* skip permission errors */ }
505	  }
506	
507	  scanDir(folderPath);
508	  return result;
509	});
510	
511	// ── IPC: Run command with streaming output ────────────────────────────────────
512	ipcMain.handle('command:run', async (event, { command, args, cwd }) => {
513	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
514	    const proc = spawn(command, args, {
515	      cwd,
516	      shell: true,
517	      env: { ...process.env },
518	    });
519	
520	    proc.stdout.on('data', (data) => {
521	      mainWindow.webContents.send('command:output', {
522	        type: 'stdout',
523	        text: data.toString(),
524	      });
525	    });
526	
527	    proc.stderr.on('data', (data) => {
528	      mainWindow.webContents.send('command:output', {
529	        type: 'stderr',
530	        text: data.toString(),
531	      });
532	    });
533	
534	    proc.on('close', (code) => {
535	      mainWindow.webContents.send('command:output', {
536	        type: 'exit',
537	        code,
538	        text: `\n[Process exited with code ${code}]\n`,
539	      });
540	      resolve({ code });
541	    });
542	  });
543	});
544	
545	// ── IPC: Window controls ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
546	ipcMain.on('window:minimize', () => mainWindow.minimize());
547	ipcMain.on('window:maximize', () => {
548	  mainWindow.isMaximized() ? mainWindow.unmaximize() : mainWindow.maximize();
549	});
550	ipcMain.on('window:close', () => mainWindow.close());
551	```

553	### Step 7.5 — Create the preload bridge
554	
555	Create `electron/preload.js`:
556	
557	```js
558	const { contextBridge, ipcRenderer } = require('electron');
559	
560	contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('electronAPI', {
561	  // Folder picker
562	  openFolder: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('dialog:openFolder'),
563	
564	  // Project scanning
565	  scanProject: (folderPath) => ipcRenderer.invoke('project:scan', folderPath),
566	
567	  // Command runner
568	  runCommand: (opts) => ipcRenderer.invoke('command:run', opts),
569	
570	  // Stream listener
571	  onCommandOutput: (cb) => ipcRenderer.on('command:output', (_, data) => cb(data)),
572	  removeCommandListeners: () => ipcRenderer.removeAllListeners('command:output'),
573	
574	  // Window controls
575	  minimizeWindow: () => ipcRenderer.send('window:minimize'),
576	  maximizeWindow: () => ipcRenderer.send('window:maximize'),
577	  closeWindow: () => ipcRenderer.send('window:close'),
578	});

636	### Step 8.2 — Create the main App component
637	
638	Create `src/App.jsx`:
639	
640	```jsx
641	import { useState, useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
642	import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';
643	import TitleBar from './components/TitleBar';
644	import ProjectDropzone from './components/ProjectDropzone';
645	import ProjectInfo from './components/ProjectInfo';
646	import Terminal from './components/Terminal';
647	import ActionButtons from './components/ActionButtons';
648	import AIScanner from './components/AIScanner';
649	import './index.css';
650	
651	export default function App() {
652	  const [projectPath, setProjectPath] = useState(null);
653	  const [scanData, setScanData] = useState(null);
654	  const [aiAnalysis, setAiAnalysis] = useState(null);
655	  const [isScanning, setIsScanning] = useState(false);
656	  const [isRunning, setIsRunning] = useState(false);
657	  const [terminalLines, setTerminalLines] = useState([
658	    { type: 'info', text: '▸ ProjectLauncher ready. Select a project folder to begin.\n' }
659	  ]);
660	  const [recentProjects, setRecentProjects] = useState(() => {
661	    try { return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('recentProjects') || '[]'); }
662	    catch { return []; }
663	  });
664	
665	  const addTerminalLine = useCallback((line) => {
666	    setTerminalLines(prev => [...prev.slice(-500), line]);
667	  }, []);
668	
669	  const handleFolderSelect = async () => {
670	    const folder = await window.electronAPI.openFolder();
671	    if (!folder) return;
672	    loadProject(folder);
673	  };
674	
675	  const loadProject = async (folder) => {
676	    setProjectPath(folder);
677	    setScanData(null);
678	    setAiAnalysis(null);
679	    setTerminalLines([]);
680	
681	    addTerminalLine({ type: 'info', text: `▸ Loading project: ${folder}\n` });
682	    setIsScanning(true);
683	
684	    const scan = await window.electronAPI.scanProject(folder);
685	    setScanData(scan);
686	
687	    addTerminalLine({
688	      type: 'success',
689	      text: `✓ Found ${scan.files.length} files | Entry: ${scan.entryPoint || 'detecting...'} | Requirements: ${scan.requirementsFile || 'none found'}\n`
690	    });
691	
692	    // Save to recent
693	    const updated = [folder, ...recentProjects.filter(p => p !== folder)].slice(0, 10);
694	    setRecentProjects(updated);
695	    localStorage.setItem('recentProjects', JSON.stringify(updated));
696	  };
697	
698	  return (
699	    <div className="h-screen w-screen bg-void text-text font-display flex flex-col overflow-hidden">
700	      <TitleBar />
701	
702	      <div className="flex flex-1 overflow-hidden">
703	        {/* Left Panel */}
704	        <div className="w-80 flex flex-col border-r border-border bg-surface p-4 gap-4 overflow-y-auto">
705	          <ProjectDropzone
706	            onFolderSelect={handleFolderSelect}
707	            onFolderDrop={loadProject}
708	            projectPath={projectPath}
709	          />
710	
711	          {recentProjects.length > 0 && (
712	            <div>
713	              <p className="text-xs text-muted uppercase tracking-widest mb-2">Recent Projects</p>
714	              {recentProjects.map(p => (
715	                <button
716	                  key={p}
717	                  onClick={() => loadProject(p)}
718	                  className="w-full text-left text-xs text-muted hover:text-accent truncate py-1 px-2 rounded hover:bg-panel transition-colors"
719	                  title={p}
720	                >
721	                  {p.split(/[\\/]/).pop()}
722	                </button>
723	              ))}
724	            </div>
725	          )}
726	        </div>
727	
728	        {/* Main Panel */}
729	        <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col overflow-hidden">
730	          <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
731	            {scanData ? (
732	              <motion.div
733	                key="content"
734	                initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 10 }}
735	                animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
736	                className="flex-1 flex flex-col overflow-hidden"
737	              >
738	                <ProjectInfo scanData={scanData} aiAnalysis={aiAnalysis} />
739	                <AIScanner
740	                  scanData={scanData}
741	                  isScanning={isScanning}
742	                  setIsScanning={setIsScanning}
743	                  setAiAnalysis={setAiAnalysis}
744	                  addTerminalLine={addTerminalLine}
745	                />
746	                <ActionButtons
747	                  scanData={scanData}
748	                  aiAnalysis={aiAnalysis}
749	                  isRunning={isRunning}
750	                  setIsRunning={setIsRunning}
751	                  addTerminalLine={addTerminalLine}
752	                />
753	                <Terminal lines={terminalLines} />
754	              </motion.div>
755	            ) : (
756	              <motion.div
757	                key="empty"
758	                initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
759	                animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
760	                className="flex-1 flex items-center justify-center"
761	              >
762	                <div className="text-center">
763	                  <div className="text-6xl mb-4">⬡</div>
764	                  <p className="text-muted text-sm">Select a project folder to get started</p>
765	                </div>
766	              </motion.div>
767	            )}
768	          </AnimatePresence>
769	        </div>
770	      </div>
771	    </div>
772	  );
773	}

812	Create `src/components/ProjectDropzone.jsx`:
813	
814	```jsx
815	import { useState } from 'react';
816	import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
817	import { FolderOpen, FolderCheck } from 'lucide-react';
818	
819	export default function ProjectDropzone({ onFolderSelect, onFolderDrop, projectPath }) {
820	  const [isDragging, setIsDragging] = useState(false);
821	
822	  const handleDrop = (e) => {
823	    e.preventDefault();
824	    setIsDragging(false);
825	    const items = Array.from(e.dataTransfer.items);
826	    for (const item of items) {
827	      const entry = item.webkitGetAsEntry?.();
828	      if (entry?.isDirectory) {
829	        // Get path from file
830	        const file = item.getAsFile();
831	        if (file?.path) onFolderDrop(file.path);
832	        return;
833	      }
834	    }
835	    const files = Array.from(e.dataTransfer.files);
836	    if (files[0]?.path) onFolderDrop(files[0].path);
837	  };
838	
839	  return (
840	    <motion.button
841	      onClick={onFolderSelect}
842	      onDragOver={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); setIsDragging(true); }}
843	      onDragLeave={() => setIsDragging(false)}
844	      onDrop={handleDrop}
845	      whileHover={{ scale: 1.01 }}
846	      whileTap={{ scale: 0.99 }}
847	      className={`
848	        w-full rounded-lg border-2 border-dashed p-6 text-center
849	        transition-all duration-200 cursor-pointer
850	        ${isDragging
851	          ? 'border-accent bg-accent/10 shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(0,212,255,0.2)]'
852	          : projectPath
853	            ? 'border-success/50 bg-success/5'
854	            : 'border-border hover:border-accent/50 hover:bg-panel'
855	        }
856	      `}
857	    >
858	      {projectPath ? (
859	        <>
860	          <FolderCheck className="w-8 h-8 text-success mx-auto mb-2" />
861	          <p className="text-success text-xs font-mono truncate">
862	            {projectPath.split(/[\\/]/).pop()}
863	          </p>
864	          <p className="text-muted text-xs mt-1">Click to change</p>
865	        </>
866	      ) : (
867	        <>
868	          <FolderOpen className="w-8 h-8 text-muted mx-auto mb-2" />
869	          <p className="text-text text-sm font-medium">Drop project folder</p>
870	          <p className="text-muted text-xs mt-1">or click to browse</p>
871	        </>
872	      )}
873	    </motion.button>
874	  );
875	}
876	```
877	
878	Create `src/components/ActionButtons.jsx`:
879	
880	```jsx
881	import { useState } from 'react';
882	import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
883	import { Download, Play, Loader2 } from 'lucide-react';
884	
885	export default function ActionButtons({ scanData, aiAnalysis, isRunning, setIsRunning, addTerminalLine }) {
886	  const [activeAction, setActiveAction] = useState(null);
887	
888	  const canInstall = scanData?.requirementsFile;
889	  const canStart = scanData?.entryPoint || aiAnalysis?.startCommand;
890	
891	  const runAction = async (type) => {
892	    if (isRunning) return;
893	    setIsRunning(true);
894	    setActiveAction(type);
895	
896	    const cwd = scanData.folderPath;
897	    let command, args, label;
898	
899	    if (type === 'install') {
900	      label = '▸ Installing requirements...';
901	      if (scanData.packageManager === 'npm') {
902	        command = 'npm'; args = ['install'];
903	      } else if (scanData.requirementsFile?.endsWith('pyproject.toml')) {
904	        command = 'pip'; args = ['install', '-e', '.'];
905	      } else {
906	        command = 'pip'; args = ['install', '-r', scanData.requirementsFile];
907	      }
908	    } else {
909	      label = '▸ Starting project...';
910	      const startCmd = aiAnalysis?.startCommand || detectStartCommand(scanData);
911	      [command, ...args] = startCmd.split(' ');
912	    }
913	
914	    addTerminalLine({ type: 'info', text: `\n${label}\n` });
915	    addTerminalLine({ type: 'cmd', text: `$ ${command} ${args.join(' ')}\n` });
916	
917	    window.electronAPI.onCommandOutput((data) => {
918	      addTerminalLine({ type: data.type, text: data.text });
919	    });
920	
921	    await window.electronAPI.runCommand({ command, args, cwd });
922	    window.electronAPI.removeCommandListeners();
923	
924	    setIsRunning(false);
925	    setActiveAction(null);
926	  };
927	
928	  return (
929	    <div className="px-4 py-3 border-b border-border bg-panel flex gap-3">
930	      <ActionButton
931	        label="Install Requirements"
932	        icon={<Download className="w-4 h-4" />}
933	        onClick={() => runAction('install')}
934	        disabled={!canInstall || isRunning}
935	        loading={activeAction === 'install'}
936	        color="accent"
937	      />
938	      <ActionButton
939	        label="Start Project"
940	        icon={<Play className="w-4 h-4" />}
941	        onClick={() => runAction('start')}
942	        disabled={!canStart || isRunning}
943	        loading={activeAction === 'start'}
944	        color="success"
945	      />
946	    </div>
947	  );
948	}
949	
950	function ActionButton({ label, icon, onClick, disabled, loading, color }) {
951	  const colors = {
952	    accent: 'border-accent/50 hover:border-accent hover:bg-accent/10 text-accent disabled:text-muted disabled:border-border',
953	    success: 'border-success/50 hover:border-success hover:bg-success/10 text-success disabled:text-muted disabled:border-border',
954	  };
955	
956	  return (
957	    <motion.button
958	      onClick={onClick}
959	      disabled={disabled}
960	      whileHover={!disabled ? { scale: 1.02 } : {}}
961	      whileTap={!disabled ? { scale: 0.98 } : {}}
962	      className={`
963	        flex items-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 rounded-lg border text-sm font-mono
964	        transition-all duration-150 disabled:opacity-40 disabled:cursor-not-allowed
965	        ${colors[color]}
966	      `}
967	    >
968	      {loading ? <Loader2 className="w-4 h-4 animate-spin" /> : icon}
969	      {label}
970	    </motion.button>
971	  );
972	}
973	
974	function detectStartCommand(scanData) {
975	  if (!scanData.entryPoint) return 'echo "No entry point found"';
976	  const ep = scanData.entryPoint;
977	  if (ep.endsWith('.py')) return `python ${ep}`;
978	  if (ep === 'manage.py') return 'python manage.py runserver';
979	  if (ep.endsWith('.js')) return `node ${ep}`;
980	  return `python ${ep}`;
981	}
982	```
983

1031	## 9. Build the AI Project Scanner Engine
1032	
1033	This is the core — where the free AI API reads your project and understands it.
1034	
1035	Create `src/components/AIScanner.jsx`:
1036	
1037	```jsx
1038	import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
1039	import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
1040	import { Brain, CheckCircle, AlertCircle } from 'lucide-react';
1041	
1042	// ── AI Provider config (reads from env) ──────────────────────────────────────
1043	const AI_PROVIDERS = {
1044	  openrouter: {
1045	    baseURL: import.meta.env.VITE_OPENROUTER_BASE_URL || 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1',
1046	    apiKey: import.meta.env.VITE_OPENROUTER_API_KEY,
1047	    model: import.meta.env.VITE_OPENROUTER_MODEL || 'google/gemini-flash-1.5',
1048	  },
1049	  gemini: {
1050	    baseURL: 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai',
1051	    apiKey: import.meta.env.VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY,
1052	    model: import.meta.env.VITE_GEMINI_MODEL || 'gemini-1.5-flash',
1053	  },
1054	  nvidia: {
1055	    baseURL: import.meta.env.VITE_NVIDIA_BASE_URL || 'https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1',
1056	    apiKey: import.meta.env.VITE_NVIDIA_API_KEY,
1057	    model: import.meta.env.VITE_NVIDIA_MODEL || 'meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct',
1058	  },
1059	};
1060	
1061	function getProvider() {
1062	  if (import.meta.env.VITE_OPENROUTER_API_KEY) return AI_PROVIDERS.openrouter;
1063	  if (import.meta.env.VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY) return AI_PROVIDERS.gemini;
1064	  if (import.meta.env.VITE_NVIDIA_API_KEY) return AI_PROVIDERS.nvidia;
1065	  return null;
1066	}
1067	
1068	export default function AIScanner({ scanData, isScanning, setIsScanning, setAiAnalysis, addTerminalLine }) {
1069	  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle'); // idle | scanning | done | error | no-api
1070	  const [analysis, setAnalysis] = useState(null);
1071	
1072	  useEffect(() => {
1073	    if (scanData && isScanning) {
1074	      runAIScan();
1075	    }
1076	  }, [scanData]);
1077	
1078	  const runAIScan = async () => {
1079	    const provider = getProvider();
1080	
1081	    if (!provider || !provider.apiKey) {
1082	      setStatus('no-api');
1083	      setIsScanning(false);
1084	      addTerminalLine({ type: 'warning', text: '⚠ No AI API key configured. Using file-based detection only.\n' });
1085	      // Fall back to heuristic detection
1086	      const fallback = heuristicAnalysis(scanData);
1087	      setAiAnalysis(fallback);
1088	      return;
1089	    }
1090	
1091	    setStatus('scanning');
1092	    addTerminalLine({ type: 'info', text: `▸ AI scanning project with ${provider.model}...\n` });
1093	
1094	    try {
1095	      const prompt = buildScanPrompt(scanData);
1096	
1097	      const response = await fetch(`${provider.baseURL}/chat/completions`, {
1098	        method: 'POST',
1099	        headers: {
1100	          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
1101	          'Authorization': `Bearer ${provider.apiKey}`,
1102	          'HTTP-Referer': 'projectlauncher-app',
1103	        },
1104	        body: JSON.stringify({
1105	          model: provider.model,
1106	          max_tokens: 800,
1107	          response_format: { type: 'json_object' },
1108	          messages: [
1109	            {
1110	              role: 'system',
1111	              content: 'You are a project analysis AI. Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No markdown, no explanation.',
1112	            },
1113	            { role: 'user', content: prompt },
1114	          ],
1115	        }),
1116	      });
1117	
1118	      if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`API error: ${response.status}`);
1119	
1120	      const data = await response.json();
1121	      const text = data.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || '{}';
1122	      const result = JSON.parse(text);
1123	
1124	      setAnalysis(result);
1125	      setAiAnalysis(result);
1126	      setStatus('done');
1127	      addTerminalLine({ type: 'success', text: `✓ AI analysis complete. Project type: ${result.projectType || 'unknown'}\n` });
1128	      if (result.startCommand) {
1129	        addTerminalLine({ type: 'info', text: `  Start command: ${result.startCommand}\n` });
1130	      }
1131	      if (result.description) {
1132	        addTerminalLine({ type: 'muted', text: `  ${result.description}\n` });
1133	      }
1134	
1135	    } catch (err) {
1136	      setStatus('error');
1137	      addTerminalLine({ type: 'stderr', text: `✗ AI scan failed: ${err.message}. Using heuristics.\n` });
1138	      const fallback = heuristicAnalysis(scanData);
1139	      setAiAnalysis(fallback);
1140	    }
1141	
1142	    setIsScanning(false);
1143	  };
1144	
1145	  return (
1146	    <div className="px-4 py-2 bg-panel border-b border-border flex items-center gap-3">
1147	      <Brain className={`w-4 h-4 ${
1148	        status === 'scanning' ? 'text-accent animate-pulse' :
1149	        status === 'done' ? 'text-success' :
1150	        status === 'error' ? 'text-danger' : 'text-muted'
1151	      }`} />
1152	      <span className="text-xs font-mono text-muted">
1153	        {status === 'idle' && 'AI Scanner ready'}
1154	        {status === 'scanning' && 'Scanning project with AI...'}
1155	        {status === 'done' && `AI Analysis complete — ${analysis?.projectType || 'Python'} project detected`}
1156	        {status === 'error' && 'AI scan failed — using file detection'}
1157	        {status === 'no-api' && 'No API key — using file detection'}
1158	      </span>
1159	    </div>
1160	  );
1161	}
1162	
1163	// Build a lean prompt that stays under free tier limits
1164	function buildScanPrompt(scanData) {
1165	  const files = scanData.files.slice(0, 40).join('\n');
1166	  return `Analyze this project and respond with JSON only.
1167	
1168	Project files:
1169	${files}
1170	
1171	Entry point detected: ${scanData.entryPoint || 'unknown'}
1172	Requirements file: ${scanData.requirementsFile || 'none'}
1173	
1174	Return JSON with these fields:
1175	{
1176	  "projectType": "django|flask|fastapi|streamlit|pytorch|tensorflow|nodejs|react|vue|unknown",
1177	  "description": "one sentence description",
1178	  "startCommand": "exact command to run the project",
1179	  "installCommand": "exact command to install dependencies",
1180	  "port": null or port number if applicable,
1181	  "notes": "any important notes about running this project"
1182	}`;
1183	}
1184	
1185	// Fallback when no AI key
1186	function heuristicAnalysis(scanData) {
1187	  const files = scanData.files.map(f => f.toLowerCase());
1188	  const has = (name) => files.some(f => f.includes(name));
1189	
1190	  let projectType = 'unknown';
1191	  let startCommand = null;
1192	
1193	  if (has('manage.py')) { projectType = 'django'; startCommand = 'python manage.py runserver'; }
1194	  else if (has('app.py') && has('flask')) { projectType = 'flask'; startCommand = 'python app.py'; }
1195	  else if (has('streamlit')) { projectType = 'streamlit'; startCommand = `streamlit run ${scanData.entryPoint}`; }
1196	  else if (has('main.py')) { projectType = 'python'; startCommand = 'python main.py'; }
1197	  else if (has('package.json')) {
1198	    projectType = 'nodejs';
1199	    startCommand = has('next.config') ? 'npm run dev' : 'node index.js';
1200	  }
1201	
1202	  return { projectType, startCommand, description: `${projectType} project`, notes: 'Detected via file analysis' };
1203	}
1204	```
1205

1231	## 10. Wire Up: Install Requirements Button
1232	
1233	The button logic is already in `ActionButtons.jsx` from Section 8.
1234	
1235	Here are the detection rules it follows:
1236	
1237	| File Found | Command Run |
1238	|---|---|
1239	| `requirements.txt` | `pip install -r requirements.txt` |
1240	| `pyproject.toml` | `pip install -e .` |
1241	| `package.json` | `npm install` |
1242	| `yarn.lock` | `yarn install` |
1243	| `Pipfile` | `pipenv install` |
1244	
1245	To enhance detection, add this to `electron/main.js` in the scan handler:
1246	
1247	```js
1248	// Enhanced package manager detection
1249	if (entries.find(e => e.name === 'yarn.lock')) result.packageManager = 'yarn';
1250	if (entries.find(e => e.name === 'Pipfile')) result.packageManager = 'pipenv';
1251	if (entries.find(e => e.name === 'poetry.lock')) result.packageManager = 'poetry';
1252	if (entries.find(e => e.name === 'Cargo.toml')) result.packageManager = 'cargo';
1253	```

1257	## 11. Wire Up: Start Project Button
1258	
1259	The start command comes from two sources (in priority order):
1260	
1261	1. **AI Analysis** — the AI tells us `startCommand` like `uvicorn main:app --reload`
1262	2. **Heuristic** — we detect from file names
1263	
1264	The button is already wired in `ActionButtons.jsx`. The AI scanner sets `aiAnalysis.startCommand`.
1265

1267

1270	else if (has('uvicorn') || has('fastapi')) { projectType = 'fastapi'; startCommand = 'uvicorn main:app --reload'; }
1271	else if (has('gradio')) { projectType = 'gradio'; startCommand = 'python app.py'; }
1272	else if (has('next.config')) { projectType = 'nextjs'; startCommand = 'npm run dev'; }
1273	else if (has('vite.config')) { projectType = 'vite'; startCommand = 'npm run dev'; }
1274	```

1336	## 14. Dot Mac (.mdc) Rules Configuration
1337	
1338	`.mdc` (Markdown Config) files tell Claude Code and Roo Code how to behave in your project.
1339	
1340	### Step 14.1 — Create the rules folder
1341	
1342	```bash
1343	mkdir -p .cursor/rules
1344	# Also works as:
1345	mkdir -p .claude/rules
1346	```
1347	
1348	### Step 14.2 — Create your main project rule
1349	
1350	Create `.cursor/rules/project.mdc`:
1351	
1352	```markdown
1353	---
1354	description: ProjectLauncher — AI Rules
1355	globs: ["**/*.jsx", "**/*.js", "electron/**/*"]
1356	---
1357	
1358	# ProjectLauncher Development Rules
1359	
1360	## Architecture
1361	- Electron main process: `electron/main.js` — Node.js only, no React imports
1362	- Renderer: `src/` — React + Vite, no direct Node.js (use IPC via window.electronAPI)
1363	- Bridge: `electron/preload.js` — contextBridge only
1364	
1365	## Code Style
1366	- Use Tailwind for ALL styling — no inline styles except dynamic values
1367	- Use framer-motion for ALL animations
1368	- Use lucide-react for ALL icons
1369	- All components in `src/components/`
1370	- All IPC handlers in `electron/main.js`
1371	
1372	## Security Rules
1373	- NEVER use nodeIntegration: true
1374	- NEVER disable contextIsolation
1375	- NEVER expose shell or fs directly to renderer
1376	- All file operations go through IPC handlers
1377	
1378	## AI API Rules
1379	- API keys MUST be in .env with VITE_ prefix
1380	- NEVER hardcode API keys
1381	- Always include fallback heuristic analysis
1382	
1383	## Naming
1384	- Components: PascalCase
1385	- Files: PascalCase for components, camelCase for utilities
1386	- IPC channels: namespace:action format (e.g., dialog:openFolder)
1387	```
1388	
1389	Create `.cursor/rules/ui.mdc`:
1390	
1391	```markdown
1392	---
1393	description: UI/UX Design Rules
1394	globs: ["src/**/*.jsx", "src/**/*.css"]
1395	---
1396	
1397	# UI Design System Rules
1398	
1399	## Color Palette (MUST follow)
1400	- Background: #0a0a0f (void)
1401	- Surface: #111118
1402	- Panel: #1a1a24
1403	- Border: #2a2a3a
1404	- Accent: #00d4ff
1405	- Success: #00ff88
1406	- Warning: #ffaa00
1407	- Danger: #ff3366
1408	- Text: #e0e0f0
1409	- Muted: #666688
1410	
1411	## Typography
1412	- Display/UI: Space Grotesk
1413	- Code/Terminal: JetBrains Mono
1414	- ALL terminal output uses font-mono class
1415	
1416	## Component Rules
1417	- All buttons must have hover AND active states
1418	- Disabled states use opacity-40
1419	- Loading states use animate-spin on Loader2 icon
1420	- ALL interactive elements have transition-all duration-150
1421	
1422	## Do NOT
1423	- Use rounded-full on rectangular elements
1424	- Use white backgrounds
1425	- Use system default fonts
1426	- Add box shadows larger than 24px
1427	```
1428

1430	
1431	1. Open VS Code
1432	2. Click Roo Code icon in sidebar
1433	3. Go to **Settings** → **Rules**
1434	4. Add path: `.cursor/rules/`
1435	5. Roo Code will now follow these rules automatically

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository Overview

This is a skills repository for Office document manipulation (PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, PDF). Each skill provides workflows, scripts, and documentation for working with specific file formats. The repository follows a pattern established in `skills-system.md` which defines mandatory workflows for document creation and editing.

## Repository Structure

```
public/
├── pptx/           # PowerPoint presentation skills
│   ├── SKILL.md    # Main workflow documentation
│   ├── ooxml.md    # OOXML editing guide
│   ├── html2pptx.md # HTML-to-PPTX conversion guide
│   ├── scripts/    # Python and JS utilities
│   └── ooxml/      # OOXML validation and schemas
├── docx/           # Word document skills
│   ├── SKILL.md    # Main workflow documentation
│   ├── ooxml.md    # OOXML editing guide
│   ├── docx-js.md  # JavaScript library documentation
│   └── scripts/    # Python utilities
├── pdf/            # PDF manipulation skills
│   ├── SKILL.md    # Main workflow documentation
│   ├── REFERENCE.md # Advanced features and examples
│   └── FORMS.md    # PDF form filling guide
└── xlsx/           # Excel spreadsheet skills
    └── SKILL.md    # Main workflow documentation

outputs/            # All skill-generated documents (gitignored)
└── <document-name>/ # One directory per document project
    ├── *.pptx      # Final outputs
    ├── *.docx      # Final outputs
    ├── *.pdf       # Final outputs
    ├── *.xlsx      # Final outputs
    ├── unpacked/   # Unpacked OOXML files
    ├── *.json      # Inventories and replacements
    ├── *.html      # HTML slides
    └── images/     # Generated images
```

## Key Architecture Principles

### Skills-Based System
The repository follows a mandatory skills-check system (defined in `skills-system.md`):
1. **Before writing ANY code**: Check if a skill exists for the task
2. **If YES**: Read the corresponding SKILL.md and follow it exactly
3. **If NO**: Only then proceed with custom code

This prevents reinventing workflows that already exist in the skills documentation.

### Two-Phase Approach for Complex Operations
Most OOXML editing workflows follow this pattern:
1. **Unpack**: Extract the Office file to raw XML using `venv/bin/python ooxml/scripts/unpack.py`
2. **Edit**: Modify XML files directly
3. **Validate**: Check changes using `venv/bin/python ooxml/scripts/validate.py --original <file>`
4. **Pack**: Repackage to Office file using `venv/bin/python ooxml/scripts/pack.py`

### Read-First Policy
All SKILL.md files must be read **completely** before starting work. Never set range limits when reading these files - they contain critical workflow steps and validation requirements.

### Output Directory Convention
**MANDATORY**: All files created or edited using any skill workflow MUST be written to:
```
outputs/<fitting-name-for-document>/
```

Where `<fitting-name-for-document>` is a descriptive, lowercase, hyphenated name for the document being worked on.

**Examples**:
- `outputs/quarterly-sales-report/` - for a sales presentation
- `outputs/employee-handbook/` - for a Word document
- `outputs/budget-2024/` - for an Excel file
- `outputs/project-proposal/` - for a PDF document

**Rules**:
1. Create the outputs directory if it doesn't exist
2. All intermediate files (unpacked XML, JSON inventories, HTML files, images, etc.) go in this directory
3. Final output files (PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, PDF) go in this directory
4. Never write skill-generated files to the repository root or public/ directories
5. Use descriptive names that clearly identify the document's purpose

## Development Setup

### Python Environment
The repository uses a Python virtual environment:

```bash
# Install/update dependencies
venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt
```

**CRITICAL**: Always use `venv/bin/python` for all Python commands. NEVER use system Python or assume venv is activated. All Python commands in this document use explicit venv paths.

### Node.js Dependencies
JavaScript tools for html2pptx workflow:

```bash
# Install dependencies (includes playwright chromium browser)
npm install
```

### System Tools
Required system dependencies (should be pre-installed):
- LibreOffice: `soffice` (for PDF conversion)
- Poppler: `pdftoppm` (for PDF to image conversion)
- Pandoc: `pandoc` (for document text extraction)

## Common Commands

### PowerPoint (PPTX)

**Text extraction**:
```bash
venv/bin/python -m markitdown file.pptx
```

**Unpack for XML editing**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/pptx/ooxml/scripts/unpack.py input.pptx outputs/<document-name>/unpacked/
```

**Validate after editing**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/pptx/ooxml/scripts/validate.py outputs/<document-name>/unpacked/ --original input.pptx
```

**Repack to PPTX**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/pptx/ooxml/scripts/pack.py outputs/<document-name>/unpacked/ outputs/<document-name>/final.pptx
```

**Create thumbnail grid for visual analysis**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/thumbnail.py template.pptx outputs/<document-name>/thumbnails [--cols 4]
```

**Rearrange slides (duplicate, reorder, delete)**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/rearrange.py template.pptx outputs/<document-name>/rearranged.pptx 0,5,5,12,3
```

**Extract text inventory**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/inventory.py presentation.pptx outputs/<document-name>/inventory.json
```

**Replace text from JSON**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/replace.py input.pptx outputs/<document-name>/replacements.json outputs/<document-name>/output.pptx
```

**Convert HTML to PPTX** (requires Node.js):
```bash
node script.js  # Uses html2pptx.js library
```

### Word Documents (DOCX)

**Text extraction with tracked changes**:
```bash
pandoc --track-changes=all file.docx -o outputs/<document-name>/extracted.md
```

**Unpack/Validate/Pack**: Same pattern as PPTX above (use `outputs/<document-name>/` directory)

### PDF

**Merge PDFs**:
```python
from pypdf import PdfWriter, PdfReader
writer = PdfWriter()
for pdf in ["doc1.pdf", "doc2.pdf"]:
    reader = PdfReader(pdf)
    for page in reader.pages:
        writer.add_page(page)
with open("outputs/<document-name>/merged.pdf", "wb") as f:
    writer.write(f)
```

**Convert PPTX to PDF** (for visual analysis):
```bash
soffice --headless --convert-to pdf --outdir outputs/<document-name>/ presentation.pptx
```

**Convert PDF to images**:
```bash
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 file.pdf outputs/<document-name>/page
```

### Excel (XLSX)

See `public/xlsx/SKILL.md` for comprehensive formula and formatting standards. Key principles:
- Zero formula errors required
- Color coding: Blue=inputs, Black=formulas, Green=internal links, Red=external links
- Format zeros as "-" in number formatting
- Years as text strings, not numbers

## Critical Workflow Notes

### PPTX Creation from Scratch
1. Create output directory: `mkdir -p outputs/<document-name>/`
2. Read `public/pptx/html2pptx.md` completely (no range limits)
3. Design content-informed color palettes (don't use defaults blindly)
4. Create HTML files for each slide in `outputs/<document-name>/` (720pt × 405pt for 16:9)
5. Use `class="placeholder"` for charts/tables to be added via PptxGenJS
6. Rasterize gradients and icons as PNG using Sharp, save to `outputs/<document-name>/images/`
7. Generate presentation to `outputs/<document-name>/presentation.pptx` using `html2pptx.js` library
8. Create thumbnail grid and validate visually for text cutoff, overlap, positioning issues
9. Iterate until all slides are visually correct

### PPTX Creation from Template
1. Create output directory: `mkdir -p outputs/<document-name>/`
2. Extract text: `venv/bin/python -m markitdown template.pptx`
3. Create thumbnail grid: `venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/thumbnail.py template.pptx outputs/<document-name>/template`
4. Analyze template and save inventory to `outputs/<document-name>/template-inventory.md` (list ALL slides with 0-based indices)
5. Create outline with template mapping (verify slide indices are within range)
6. Rearrange slides: `venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/rearrange.py template.pptx outputs/<document-name>/working.pptx 0,34,34,50,52`
7. Extract text inventory: `venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/inventory.py outputs/<document-name>/working.pptx outputs/<document-name>/text-inventory.json`
8. Read entire `text-inventory.json` (no range limits)
9. Generate replacement JSON to `outputs/<document-name>/replacements.json` with proper paragraph formatting (bold, bullets, alignment, colors)
10. Apply replacements: `venv/bin/python public/pptx/scripts/replace.py outputs/<document-name>/working.pptx outputs/<document-name>/replacements.json outputs/<document-name>/final.pptx`

**CRITICAL**: Shapes not listed in replacement JSON are automatically cleared. Only shapes with "paragraphs" field get new content.

### DOCX Editing
- **Someone else's document or formal docs**: Use "Redlining workflow" (tracked changes)
- **Your own document + simple changes**: Use "Basic OOXML editing"
- Always preserve existing formatting and document structure

### File Organization
All skill-based workflows follow the **Output Directory Convention** (see Key Architecture Principles above):
- Working files → `outputs/<document-name>/`
- Final outputs → `outputs/<document-name>/`
- Never commit outputs to git (outputs/ is gitignored)

## Validation is Mandatory

After any OOXML editing (PPTX/DOCX), **always validate immediately**:
```bash
venv/bin/python public/[format]/ooxml/scripts/validate.py <dir> --original <file>
```

Fix validation errors before proceeding. Never pack a file without validating first.

## Dependencies

### Python Packages (requirements.txt)
- **Office formats**: `python-pptx`, `openpyxl`, `pypdf`
- **XML processing**: `defusedxml`, `lxml`
- **Images**: `Pillow`, `pdf2image`
- **Conversion**: `markitdown`
- **Utilities**: `six`

### Node.js Packages (package.json)
- **Presentation generation**: `pptxgenjs` (v4.0.1)
- **HTML rendering**: `playwright` (includes Chromium)
- **Image processing**: `sharp`
- **Icons**: `react`, `react-dom`, `react-icons`

### System Tools
- **LibreOffice**: `soffice` - PPTX to PDF conversion
- **Poppler**: `pdftoppm` - PDF to image conversion
- **Pandoc**: `pandoc` - Document text extraction with tracked changes

## Code Style

When generating code for document operations:
- Write concise code
- Avoid verbose variable names
- Minimize print statements
- Follow existing patterns in scripts/

attachment.content:
- ruflo-core:ruflo-status: Show Ruflo system health, MCP server status, and active agents
- ruflo-core:witness: Manage and verify a cryptographically-signed fix manifest with temporal history (ADR-103)
- ruflo-swarm:swarm: Initialize, monitor, and manage multi-agent swarms
- ruflo-swarm:watch: Live-stream swarm events and agent activity in real time
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-status: Quick autopilot progress summary with task completion stats
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot: Enable, configure, or disable autonomous task completion
- ruflo-federation:federation: Manage cross-installation agent federation
- claude-mem:babysit: Watch a pull request or review cycle until it is ready to merge. Use when asked to babysit, monitor, or keep checking PR comments, reviews, and CI until all actionable issues are resolved.
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create a detailed, phased implementation plan with documentation discovery. Use when asked to plan a feature, task, or multi-step implementation — especially before executing with do.
- claude-mem:do: Execute a phased implementation plan using subagents. Use when asked to execute, run, or carry out a plan — especially one created by make-plan.
- claude-mem:how-it-works: Explain how claude-mem captures observations, when memory injection kicks in, and where data lives. Use when the user asks "how does claude-mem work?" or "what is this thing doing?".
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- claude-mem:pathfinder: Map a codebase into feature-grouped flowcharts, identify duplicated concerns across features, and propose a unified architecture. Use when asked to "find the ideal path," unify duplicated systems, or audit architecture before a refactor. Emits a proposed unified flowchart plus per-system /make-plan prompts.
- claude-mem:version-bump: Automated semantic versioning and release workflow for Claude Code plugins. Handles version increments across package.json, marketplace.json, plugin.json manifests, npm publishing (so `npx claude-mem@X.Y.Z` resolves), build verification, git tagging, GitHub releases, and changelog generation.
- claude-mem:learn-codebase: Prime a codebase by reading every source file in full. Use when starting work on a new or unfamiliar project, or when the user asks to "learn the codebase", "read the codebase", "prime", or "get up to speed".
- claude-mem:knowledge-agent: Build and query AI-powered knowledge bases from claude-mem observations. Use when users want to create focused "brains" from their observation history, ask questions about past work patterns, or compile expertise on specific topics.
- claude-mem:smart-explore: Token-optimized structural code search using tree-sitter AST parsing. Use instead of reading full files when you need to understand code structure, find functions, or explore a codebase efficiently.
- claude-mem:wowerpoint: Turn one document into a kawaii NotebookLM slide-deck PDF. Use for "wowerpoint this", "make a deck about <file>", "turn this report into slides", or any request to render a single document as shareable narrative slides.
- claude-mem:timeline-report: Generate a "Journey Into [Project]" narrative report analyzing a project's entire development history from claude-mem's timeline. Use when asked for a timeline report, project history analysis, development journey, or full project report.
- ruflo-core:ruflo-doctor: Run health checks on the Ruflo installation and fix common issues
- ruflo-core:init-project: Initialize a new Ruflo project with MCP tools, hooks, and agent configuration
- ruflo-core:discover-plugins: Discover and recommend ruflo plugins based on your workflow, installed MCP tools, and current task
- ruflo-core:witness: Sign, verify, and track fix-marker regressions over time using a deterministic Ed25519 witness manifest. Works in any project — clone the toolkit, run init, register fixes, regen on each release.
- ruflo-swarm:monitor-stream: Stream live swarm events using the Monitor tool for real-time observability
- ruflo-swarm:swarm-init: Initialize a multi-agent swarm with anti-drift configuration
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-loop: Run an autonomous /loop iteration -- check progress, work on next task, schedule next wake
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-predict: Use learned patterns and current state to predict the optimal next action
- ruflo-federation:federation-init: Initialize federation on this node — generate keypair and configure peers
- ruflo-federation:federation-audit: Query federation audit logs with compliance filtering
- ruflo-federation:federation-status: Show federation health — peers, sessions, trust levels, and message metrics
- ui-ux-pro-max:ui-ux-pro-max: UI/UX design intelligence for web and mobile. Includes 50+ styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 10 stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and HTML/CSS). Actions: plan, build, create, design, implement, review, fix, improve, optimize, enhance, refactor, and check UI/UX code. Projects: website, landing page, dashboard, admin panel, e-commerce, SaaS, portfolio, blog, and mobile app. Elements: button, modal, navbar, sidebar, card, table, form, and chart. Styles: glassmorphism, claymorphism, minimalism, brutalism, neumorphism, bento grid, dark mode, responsive, skeuomorphism, and flat design. Topics: color systems, accessibility, animation, layout, typography, font pairing, spacing, interaction states, shadow, and gradient. Integrations: shadcn/ui MCP for component search and examples.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Self-Learning Hooks System

Intelligent workflow automation with pattern learning and adaptive routing

Subcommands:
  - pre-edit        - Get context before editing files
  - post-edit       - Record editing outcomes for learning
  - pre-command     - Assess risk before executing commands
  - post-command    - Record command execution outcomes
  - pre-task        - Record task start and get agent suggestions
  - post-task       - Record task completion for learning
  - session-end     - End current session and persist state
  - session-restore - Restore a previous session
  - route           - Route tasks to optimal agents
  - explain         - Explain routing decisions
  - pretrain        - Bootstrap intelligence from repository
  - build-agents    - Generate optimized agent configs
  - metrics         - View learning metrics dashboard
  - transfer        - Transfer patterns from another project
  - list            - List all registered hooks
  - worker          - Background worker management (12 workers)
  - progress        - Check V3 implementation progress
  - statusline      - Generate dynamic statusline display
  - coverage-route  - Route tasks based on coverage gaps (ruvector)
  - coverage-suggest- Suggest coverage improvements
  - coverage-gaps   - List all coverage gaps with agents

  - model-route    - Route to optimal model (haiku/sonnet/opus)
  - model-outcome  - Record model routing outcome
  - model-stats    - View model routing statistics
  -

  - teammate-idle  - Handle idle teammate (auto-assign tasks)

{
  "name": "project-launcher",
  "private": true,
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "description": "AI-powered project launcher",
  "main": "electron/main.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "concurrently \"npm run dev:vite\" \"npm run dev:electron\"",
    "dev:vite": "vite",
    "dev:electron": "wait-on http://localhost:5173 && cross-env NODE_ENV=development electron .",
    "build": "vite build",
    "build:app": "npm run build && electron-builder",
    "preview": "vite preview"
  },
  "build": {
    "appId": "com.yourdomain.projectlauncher",
    "productName": "ProjectLauncher",
    "directories": { "output": "release" },
    "files": ["dist/**/*", "electron/**/*"],
    "win": { "target": "nsis", "icon": "electron/assets/icon.ico" },
    "mac": { "target": "dmg", "icon": "electron/assets/icon.icns" },
    "linux": { "target": "AppImage", "icon": "electron/assets/icon.png" }
  }
}

content.thinking:
Wait - Tailwind CSS v4 was installed. The guide uses Tailwind v3-style configuration (tailwind.config.js with `content` array, etc.). Tailwind v4 has a completely different configuration approach using CSS-based configuration. I need to handle this carefully.

Tailwind v4 uses `@import "tailwindcss"` instead of `@tailwind base/components/utilities` directives, and configuration is done via CSS `@theme` blocks rather than `tailwind.config.js`.

Let me check the exact version and see if I should install v3 specifically, or adapt the code for v4.

Actually, let me install a specific version of Tailwind v3 to keep things simpler and match the guide. Let me install tailwindcss@3, postcss@8, autoprefixer@10.

Wait, I already have postcss@8.5 and autoprefixer@10.5. So I just need tailwindcss@3.

Let me install tailwindcss@3 and remove the v4 version.

Actually, let me just work with what we have. Tailwind v4 is the latest and it's better to use the modern approach. The configuration will be different though.

In Tailwind v4:
- No `tailwind.config.js` needed (configuration goes in CSS)
- No `postcss.config.js` needed in most setups
- Use `@import "tailwindcss"` in CSS
- Custom values use `@theme` directive in CSS
- The `@tailwindcss/typography` plugin is installed differently

Let me think about this more carefully. Actually, for simplicity and to match the guide's approach exactly, let me downgrade to Tailwind v3.

Let me install tailwindcss@3 explicitly.

import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  server: { port: 5173 },
  envPrefix: 'VITE_',
  base: './',
})

# Pick ONE provider and fill it in

# OpenRouter (recommended)
VITE_OPENROUTER_API_KEY=
VITE_OPENROUTER_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
VITE_OPENROUTER_MODEL=google/gemini-flash-1.5

# OR Gemini
VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY=
VITE_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-1.5-flash

# OR NVIDIA NIM
VITE_NVIDIA_API_KEY=
VITE_NVIDIA_BASE_URL=https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1
VITE_NVIDIA_MODEL=meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct

# App settings
VITE_MAX_SCAN_DEPTH=5
VITE_MAX_FILES_TO_SCAN=50

content.content:
# TypeScript/JavaScript Hooks

> This file extends [common/hooks.md](../common/hooks.md) with TypeScript/JavaScript specific content.

## PostToolUse Hooks

Configure in `~/.claude/settings.json`:

- **Prettier**: Auto-format JS/TS files after edit
- **TypeScript check**: Run `tsc` after editing `.ts`/`.tsx` files
- **console.log warning**: Warn about `console.log` in edited files

## Stop Hooks

- **console.log audit**: Check all modified files for `console.log` before session ends
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "**/*.ts"
  - "**/*.tsx"
  - "**/*.js"
  - "**/*.jsx"
---

# TypeScript/JavaScript Security

> This file extends [common/security.md](../common/security.md) with TypeScript/JavaScript specific content.

## Secret Management

```typescript
// NEVER: Hardcoded secrets
const apiKey = "sk-proj-xxxxx"

// ALWAYS: Environment variables
const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY

if (!apiKey) {
  throw new Error('OPENAI_API_KEY not configured')
}
```

## Agent Support

- Use **security-reviewer** skill for comprehensive security audits

attachment.content:
Self-Learning Hooks System

Intelligent workflow automation with pattern learning and adaptive routing

Usage: claude-flow hooks <subcommand> [options]

Subcommands:
  - pre-edit        - Get context before editing files
  - post-edit       - Record editing outcomes for learning
  - pre-command     - Assess risk before executing commands
  - post-command    - Record command execution outcomes
  - pre-task        - Record task start and get agent suggestions
  - post-task       - Record task completion for learning
  - session-end     - End current session and persist state
  - session-restore - Restore a previous session
  - route           - Route tasks to optimal agents
  - explain         - Explain routing decisions
  - pretrain        - Bootstrap intelligence from repository
  - build-agents    - Generate optimized agent configs
  - metrics         - View learning metrics dashboard
  - transfer        - Transfer patterns from another project
  - list            - List all registered hooks
  - worker          - Background worker management (12 workers)
  - progress        - Check V3 implementation progress
  - statusline      - Generate dynamic statusline display
  - coverage-route  - Route tasks based on coverage gaps (ruvector)
  - coverage-suggest- Suggest coverage improvements
  - coverage-gaps   - List all coverage gaps with agents
  - token-optimize - Token optimization (agentic-flow integration)
  - model-route    - Route to optimal model (haiku/sonnet/opus)
  - model-outcome  - Record model routing outcome
  - model-stats    - View model routing statistics
  - 
  - Agent Teams:
  - teammate-idle  - Handle idle teammate (auto-assign tasks)
  - task-completed - Handle task completion (train patterns)

Run "claude-flow hooks <subcommand> --help" for subcommand help

V3 Features:
  - 🧠 ReasoningBank adaptive learning
  - ⚡ Flash Attention (2.49x-7.47x speedup)
  - 🔍 AgentDB integration (150x faster search)
  - 📊 84.8% SWE-Bench solve rate
  - 🎯 32.3% token reduction
  - 🚀 2.8-4.4x speed improvement
  - 👥 Agent Teams integration (auto task assignment)
